174	
175	# ── ConclusionsRegistry ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
176	
177	class ConclusionsRegistry:
178	    """
179	    Persistent store for substantive conclusions with resolution tracking.
180	
181	    Provides methods to:
182	    - Register new conclusions
183	    - Record resolution outcomes
184	    - Query conclusions by method, domain, or status
185	    - Calculate accuracy metrics for methods
186	    - Generate calibration feedback
187	    """
188	
189	    def __init__(self, data_path: str = "conclusions_registry.json"):
190	        """
191	        Initialize the registry.
192	
193	        Args:
194	            data_path: Path to persistent JSON store
195	        """
196	        self.data_path = Path(data_path)
197	        self.conclusions: Dict[str, SubstantiveConclusion] = {}
198	        self.open_questions: Dict[str, OpenQuestionCandidate] = {}
199	        self.load()
200	
201	    def register_open_question(self, oq: OpenQuestionCandidate) -> str:
202	        """Persist an unresolved-coherence open question for founder review."""
203	        self.open_questions[oq.id] = oq
204	        self.save()
205	        logger.info("Registered open question candidate %s", oq.id)
206	        return oq.id
207	
208	    def register(self, conclusion: SubstantiveConclusion) -> str:
209	        """
210	        Register a new substantive conclusion.
211	
212	        Args:
213	            conclusion: SubstantiveConclusion object to register
214	
215	        Returns:
216	            The conclusion's ID
217	        """
218	        if not conclusion.id:
219	            conclusion.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
220	
221	        self.conclusions[conclusion.id] = conclusion
222	        logger.info(
223	            f"Registered conclusion {conclusion.id} "
224	            f"({conclusion.method_used}:{conclusion.domain}): "
225	            f"{conclusion.text[:60]}..."
226	        )
227	        self.save()
228	        return conclusion.id
229	
230	    def resolve(
231	        self,
232	        conclusion_id: str,
233	        outcome: bool,
234	        evidence: str = ""
235	    ) -> SubstantiveConclusion:
236	        """
237	        Record whether a conclusion turned out to be correct.
238	
239	        Args:
240	            conclusion_id: ID of the conclusion
241	            outcome: True if correct, False if incorrect
242	            evidence: Supporting evidence for the resolution
243	
244	        Returns:
245	            Updated SubstantiveConclusion object
246	
247	        Raises:
248	            ValueError: If conclusion_id not found
249	        """
250	        if conclusion_id not in self.conclusions:
251	            raise ValueError(f"Conclusion {conclusion_id} not found")
252	
253	        conclusion = self.conclusions[conclusion_id]
254	        conclusion.resolved = outcome
255	        conclusion.resolution_evidence = evidence
256	
257	        logger.info(
258	            f"Resolved conclusion {conclusion_id}: "
259	            f"{'CORRECT' if outcome else 'INCORRECT'}"
260	        )
261	        self.save()
262	        return conclusion
263	
264	    def get_by_method(self, method: str) -> List[SubstantiveConclusion]:
265	        """
266	        Get all conclusions produced by a given reasoning method.
267	
268	        Args:
269	            method: ReasoningMethod enum value (as string)
270	
271	        Returns:
272	            List of conclusions using that method
273	        """
274	        return [
275	            c for c in self.conclusions.values()
276	            if str(c.method_used) == method
277	        ]
278	
279	    def get_by_domain(self, domain: str) -> List[SubstantiveConclusion]:
280	        """
281	        Get all conclusions in a specific domain.
282	
283	        Args:
284	            domain: Domain name (e.g., "technology", "economics")
285	
286	        Returns:
287	            List of conclusions in that domain
288	        """
289	        return [c for c in self.conclusions.values() if c.domain == domain]
290	
291	    def get_unresolved(self) -> List[SubstantiveConclusion]:
292	        """
293	        Get all conclusions awaiting resolution.
294	
295	        Returns:
296	            List of unresolved conclusions
297	        """
298	        return [c for c in self.conclusions.values() if c.resolved is None]
299	
300	    def get_predictions_due(
301	        self,
302	        as_of: Optional[date] = None
303	    ) -> List[SubstantiveConclusion]:
304	        """
305	        Get predictions whose resolution date has passed but aren't resolved yet.
306	
307	        Args:
308	            as_of: Date to check against (default: today)
309	
310	        Returns:
311	            List of overdue predictions
312	        """
313	        if as_of is None:
314	            as_of = date.today()
315	
316	        return [
317	            c for c in self.conclusions.values()
318	            if (
319	                c.is_prediction
320	                and c.resolution_date
321	                and c.resolution_date <= as_of
322	                and c.resolved is None
323	            )
324	        ]
325	
326	    def method_accuracy(
327	        self,
328	        method: str,
329	        domain: Optional[str] = None
330	    ) -> MethodAccuracyRecord:
331	        """
332	        Calculate accuracy statistics for a reasoning method.
333	
334	        Args:
335	            method: ReasoningMethod value
336	            domain: Optional domain to filter by
337	
338	        Returns:
339	            MethodAccuracyRecord with accuracy metrics
340	        """
341	        conclusions = self.get_by_method(method)
342	        if domain:
343	            conclusions = [c for c in conclusions if c.domain == domain]
344	
345	        resolved = [c for c in conclusions if c.resolved is not None]
346	        correct = [c for c in resolved if c.resolved is True]
347	        incorrect = [c for c in resolved if c.resolved is False]
348	
349	        accuracy_rate = None
350	        brier_score = None
351	        calibration_error = None
352	
353	        if resolved:
354	            accuracy_rate = len(correct) / len(resolved)
355	
356	            # Brier score: mean squared error of confidence vs outcome
357	            brier_score = sum(
358	                (c.confidence_expressed - (1.0 if c.resolved else 0.0)) ** 2
359	                for c in resolved
360	            ) / len(resolved)
361	
362	            avg_confidence = (
363	                sum(c.confidence_expressed for c in conclusions) / len(conclusions)
364	                if conclusions else 0.0
365	            )
366	            calibration_error = abs(avg_confidence - accuracy_rate)
367	        else:
368	            avg_confidence = (
369	                sum(c.confidence_expressed for c in conclusions) / len(conclusions)
370	                if conclusions else 0.0
371	            )
372	
373	        return MethodAccuracyRecord(
374	            method_name=method,
375	            domain=domain or "all",
376	            total_conclusions=len(conclusions),
377	            resolved_conclusions=len(resolved),
378	            correct_count=len(correct),
379	            incorrect_count=len(incorrect),
380	            accuracy_rate=accuracy_rate,
381	            brier_score=brier_score,
382	            average_confidence=avg_confidence,
383	            calibration_error=calibration_error,
384	        )
385	
386	    def all_method_accuracies(self) -> List[MethodAccuracyRecord]:
387	        """
388	        Calculate accuracy breakdown by method × domain.
389	
390	        Returns:
391	            List of MethodAccuracyRecords for all combinations
392	        """
393	        records = []
394	        methods = set(str(c.method_used) for c in self.conclusions.values())
395	        domains = set(c.domain for c in self.conclusions.values())
396	
397	        # Calculate by method × domain
398	        for method in methods:
399	            for domain in domains:
400	                record = self.method_accuracy(method, domain)
401	                if record.total_conclusions > 0:
402	                    records.append(record)
403	
404	            # Also include overall by method
405	            record = self.method_accuracy(method)
406	            if record.total_conclusions > 0:
407	                records.append(record)
408	
409	        return records
410	
411	    def save(self) -> None:
412	        """Persist conclusions to JSON."""
413	        try:
414	            data = {
415	                "conclusions": [
416	                    json.loads(c.model_dump_json())
417	                    for c in self.conclusions.values()
418	                ],
419	                "open_questions": [
420	                    json.loads(o.model_dump_json())
421	                    for o in self.open_questions.values()
422	                ],
423	            }
424	            # Convert datetime objects to ISO format strings
425	            for item in data["conclusions"]:
426	                item["created_at"] = item["created_at"]
427	                if item.get("resolution_evidence"):
428	                    pass  # Already a string
429	
430	            self.data_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
431	            with open(self.data_path, 'w') as f:
432	                json.dump(data, f, indent=2, default=str)
433	            logger.info(f"Registry saved to {self.data_path}")
434	        except Exception as e:
435	            logger.error(f"Failed to save registry: {e}")
436	            raise
437	
438	    def load(self) -> None:
439	        """Load conclusions from JSON."""
440	        if not self.data_path.exists():
441	            logger.info(f"Registry file {self.data_path} not found, starting fresh")
442	            return
443	
444	        try:
445	            with open(self.data_path, 'r') as f:
446	                data = json.load(f)
447	
448	            self.conclusions = {}
449	            for item in data.get("conclusions", []):
450	                # Convert ISO datetime strings back to datetime
451	                if isinstance(item.get("created_at"), str):
452	                    item["created_at"] = datetime.fromisoformat(item["created_at"])
453	
454	                conclusion = SubstantiveConclusion(**item)
455	                self.conclusions[conclusion.id] = conclusion
456	
457	            self.open_questions = {}
458	            for item in data.get("open_questions", []):
459	                if isinstance(item.get("created_at"), str):
460	                    item["created_at"] = datetime.fromisoformat(item["created_at"])
461	                oq = OpenQuestionCandidate(**item)
462	                self.open_questions[oq.id] = oq
463	
464	            logger.info(
465	                f"Loaded {len(self.conclusions)} conclusions and "
466	                f"{len(self.open_questions)} open questions from {self.data_path}"
467	            )
468	        except Exception as e:
469	            logger.error(f"Failed to load registry: {e}")
470	            raise
471	
472	    def search(self, query: str, k: int = 10) -> List[ConclusionSummary]:
473	        """
474	        Simple text search over conclusions.
475	
476	        Args:
477	            query: Search query string
478	            k: Number of results to return
479	
480	        Returns:
481	            List of matching conclusions (up to k)
482	        """
483	        query_lower = query.lower()
484	        matches = []
485	
486	        for conclusion in self.conclusions.values():
487	            if (
488	                query_lower in conclusion.text.lower()
489	                or query_lower in conclusion.speaker_name.lower()
490	                or query_lower in conclusion.domain.lower()
491	            ):
492	                matches.append(
493	                    ConclusionSummary(
494	                        id=conclusion.id,
495	                        text=conclusion.text,
496	                        speaker_name=conclusion.speaker_name,
497	                        domain=conclusion.domain,
498	                        method_used=str(conclusion.method_used),
499	                        confidence_expressed=conclusion.confidence_expressed,
500	                        is_prediction=conclusion.is_prediction,
501	                        resolved=conclusion.resolved,
502	                        created_at=conclusion.created_at,
503	                    )
504	                )
505	
506	        # Return top k matches
507	        return matches[:k]
508	
509	
510	# ── CalibrationAnalyzer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
511	
512	class CalibrationAnalyzer:
513	    """
514	    Analyzes the firm's reasoning method performance to generate
515	    calibration feedback for the methodological brain.
516	
517	    This is the KEY integration point: it transforms substantive accuracy
518	    data into methodological observations that the main brain CAN store.
519	    """
520	
521	    def __init__(self, registry: ConclusionsRegistry):
522	        """
523	        Initialize the analyzer.
524	
525	        Args:
526	            registry: ConclusionsRegistry to analyze
527	        """
528	        self.registry = registry
529	
530	    def method_reliability_report(self) -> Dict:
531	        """
532	        Generate comprehensive reliability assessment for each reasoning method.
533	
534	        For each method the firm uses, provides:
535	        - Number of conclusions produced
536	        - Accuracy rate (where resolvable)
537	        - Average confidence vs. actual accuracy (calibration)
538	        - Domains where the method works best/worst
539	        - Actionable recommendations
540	
541	        Returns:
542	            Dictionary with reliability data for each method
543	        """
544	        accuracy_records = self.registry.all_method_accuracies()
545	
546	        # Group by method
547	        by_method: Dict[str, List[MethodAccuracyRecord]] = {}
548	        for record in accuracy_records:
549	            if record.method_name not in by_method:
550	                by_method[record.method_name] = []
551	            by_method[record.method_name].append(record)
552	
553	        report = {}
554	        for method, records in by_method.items():
555	            # Find overall record (domain="all")
556	            overall = next(
557	                (r for r in records if r.domain == "all"),
558	                None
559	            )
560	
561	            if not overall:
562	                continue
563	
564	            domain_records = [r for r in records if r.domain != "all"]
565	            domain_records.sort(
566	                key=lambda r: r.accuracy_rate if r.accuracy_rate else 0.0,
567	                reverse=True
568	            )
569	
570	            best_domains = [r.domain for r in domain_records[:3] if r.accuracy_rate]
571	            worst_domains = [r.domain for r in domain_records[-3:] if r.accuracy_rate]
572	
573	            # Determine if well-calibrated
574	            is_calibrated = False
575	            if overall.calibration_error is not None:
576	                # Well-calibrated if confidence-accuracy gap < 0.1
577	                is_calibrated = overall.calibration_error < 0.10
578	
579	            confidence_assessment = "well-calibrated"
580	            if overall.calibration_error and overall.calibration_error > 0.2:
581	                if overall.average_confidence > (overall.accuracy_rate or 0.0):
582	                    confidence_assessment = "overconfident"
583	                else:
584	                    confidence_assessment = "underconfident"
585	
586	            recommendations = self._generate_recommendations(
587	                method, overall, best_domains, worst_domains, is_calibrated
588	            )
589	
590	            report[method] = {
591	                "total_conclusions": overall.total_conclusions,
592	                "resolved_conclusions": overall.resolved_conclusions,
593	                "accuracy_rate": overall.accuracy_rate,
594	                "average_confidence": overall.average_confidence,
595	                "calibration_error": overall.calibration_error,
596	                "brier_score": overall.brier_score,
597	                "confidence_assessment": confidence_assessment,
598	                "best_domains": best_domains,
599	                "worst_domains": worst_domains,
600	                "recommendations": recommendations,
601	            }
602	
603	        return report
604	
605	    def domain_report(self, domain: str) -> Dict:
606	        """
607	        Generate performance analysis for conclusions in a specific domain.
608	
609	        For a given domain, shows:
610	        - Which methods are most/least accurate
611	        - Overall calibration
612	        - Trends over time (if available)
613	
614	        Args:
615	            domain: Domain name to analyze
616	
617	        Returns:
618	            Dictionary with domain analysis
619	        """
620	        conclusions_in_domain = self.registry.get_by_domain(domain)
621	        if not conclusions_in_domain:
622	            return {"error": f"No conclusions found in domain '{domain}'"}
623	
624	        # Group by method
625	        by_method: Dict[str, List[SubstantiveConclusion]] = {}
626	        for c in conclusions_in_domain:
627	            method = str(c.method_used)
628	            if method not in by_method:
629	                by_method[method] = []
630	            by_method[method].append(c)
631	
632	        method_analysis = {}
633	        for method, conclusions in by_method.items():
634	            resolved = [c for c in conclusions if c.resolved is not None]
635	            if resolved:
636	                correct = sum(1 for c in resolved if c.resolved)
637	                accuracy = correct / len(resolved)
638	                avg_confidence = sum(c.confidence_expressed for c in conclusions) / len(conclusions)
639	            else:
640	                accuracy = None
641	                avg_confidence = sum(c.confidence_expressed for c in conclusions) / len(conclusions)
642	
643	            method_analysis[method] = {
644	                "count": len(conclusions),
645	                "resolved": len(resolved),
646	                "accuracy": accuracy,
647	                "average_confidence": avg_confidence,
648	            }
649	
650	        # Sort by accuracy
651	        sorted_methods = sorted(
652	            method_analysis.items(),
653	            key=lambda x: x[1]["accuracy"] if x[1]["accuracy"] is not None else -1,
654	            reverse=True
655	        )
656	
657	        return {
658	            "domain": domain,
659	            "total_conclusions": len(conclusions_in_domain),
660	            "resolved_conclusions": sum(1 for c in conclusions_in_domain if c.resolved is not None),
661	            "method_breakdown": dict(sorted_methods),
662	            "best_method": sorted_methods[0][0] if sorted_methods else None,
663	            "worst_method": sorted_methods[-1][0] if sorted_methods else None,
664	        }
665	
666	    def feedback_for_methodology(self) -> List[Dict]:
667	        """
668	        Generate specific feedback for the methodological brain.
669	
670	        This is the CRITICAL integration point. It transforms substantive
671	        accuracy data into METHODOLOGICAL observations that the main brain
672	        can store and learn from.
673	
674	        Produces observations like:
675	        - "Analogical reasoning has 0.35 accuracy in tech predictions.
676	           Consider supplementing with base-rate analysis."
677	        - "First-principles reasoning has 0.78 accuracy in competitive analysis.
678	           This is a methodological strength."
679	        - "The firm is systematically overconfident (avg 0.72, accuracy 0.55).
680	           Recommend calibration training."
681	
682	        These are formatted as METHODOLOGICAL claims about which methods work,
683	        not claims about the world.
684	
685	        Returns:
686	            List of feedback dictionaries with:
687	            - feedback_type: "strength" | "weakness" | "calibration" | "combination"
688	            - method_involved: str (method name or list of methods)
689	            - observation: str (the methodological claim)
690	            - domain: str (where applicable)
691	            - confidence: float (how confident in this feedback)
692	            - evidence: dict (supporting statistics)
693	            - recommendation: str (what to do about it)
694	        """
695	        feedback = []
696	        report = self.method_reliability_report()
697	
698	        if not report:
699	            return feedback
700	
701	        # 1. Method strength/weakness feedback
702	        for method, stats in report.items():
703	            if stats["resolved_conclusions"] < 3:
704	                # Not enough data
705	                continue
706	
707	            accuracy = stats["accuracy_rate"]
708	            if accuracy is None:
709	                continue
710	
711	            # Strength if accuracy > 0.65
712	            if accuracy > 0.65:
713	                feedback.append({
714	                    "feedback_type": "strength",
715	                    "method_involved": method,
716	                    "observation": (
717	                        f"{method.replace('_', ' ').title()} reasoning demonstrates "
718	                        f"strong reliability with {accuracy:.1%} accuracy "
719	                        f"({stats['resolved_conclusions']} predictions resolved)."
720	                    ),
721	                    "domain": "all",
722	                    "confidence": min(stats["resolved_conclusions"] / 10.0, 1.0),
723	                    "evidence": {
724	                        "accuracy_rate": accuracy,
725	                        "resolved_count": stats["resolved_conclusions"],
726	                        "total_count": stats["total_conclusions"],
727	                    },
728	                    "recommendation": (
729	                        f"Increase reliance on {method.replace('_', ' ')} reasoning. "
730	                        f"Consider using it as primary method where applicable."
731	                    ),
732	                })
733	
734	            # Weakness if accuracy < 0.45
735	            elif accuracy < 0.45:
736	                feedback.append({
737	                    "feedback_type": "weakness",
738	                    "method_involved": method,
739	                    "observation": (
740	                        f"{method.replace('_', ' ').title()} reasoning shows weak reliability "
741	                        f"with only {accuracy:.1%} accuracy "
742	                        f"({stats['resolved_conclusions']} predictions resolved)."
743	                    ),
744	                    "domain": "all",
745	                    "confidence": min(stats["resolved_conclusions"] / 10.0, 1.0),
746	                    "evidence": {
747	                        "accuracy_rate": accuracy,
748	                        "resolved_count": stats["resolved_conclusions"],
749	                        "total_count": stats["total_conclusions"],
750	                    },
751	                    "recommendation": (
752	                        f"Reduce reliance on {method.replace('_', ' ')} reasoning alone. "
753	                        f"Consider combining with other methods for validation."
754	                    ),
755	                })
756	
757	        # 2. Calibration feedback
758	        overconfident_methods = [
759	            (m, s) for m, s in report.items()
760	            if s["calibration_error"] and s["calibration_error"] > 0.15
761	            and s["average_confidence"] > (s["accuracy_rate"] or 0.0)
762	        ]
763	
764	        if overconfident_methods:
765	            feedback.append({
766	                "feedback_type": "calibration",
767	                "method_involved": [m for m, s in overconfident_methods],
768	                "observation": (
769	                    f"The firm shows systematic overconfidence across "
770	                    f"{len(overconfident_methods)} reasoning methods. "
771	                    f"Average confidence exceeds actual accuracy."
772	                ),
773	                "domain": "all",
774	                "confidence": 0.8,
775	                "evidence": {
776	                    "overconfident_methods": [
777	                        {
778	                            "method": m,
779	                            "avg_confidence": s["average_confidence"],
780	                            "accuracy": s["accuracy_rate"],
781	                            "gap": s["calibration_error"],
782	                        }
783	                        for m, s in overconfident_methods
784	                    ]
785	                },
786	                "recommendation": (
787	                    "Implement confidence calibration training. "
788	                    "When expressing confidence, systematically reduce by 10-15% "
789	                    "to account for overconfidence bias."
790	                ),
791	            })
792	
793	        # 3. Domain-specific strength/weakness
794	        all_conclusions = self.registry.conclusions.values()
795	        domains = set(c.domain for c in all_conclusions)
796	
797	        for domain in domains:
798	            domain_analysis = self.domain_report(domain)
799	            if "error" in domain_analysis:
800	                continue
801	
802	            best_method = domain_analysis.get("best_method")
803	            worst_method = domain_analysis.get("worst_method")
804	
805	            if best_method:
806	                method_stats = domain_analysis["method_breakdown"][best_method]
807	                if method_stats["accuracy"] and method_stats["accuracy"] > 0.60:
808	                    feedback.append({
809	                        "feedback_type": "domain_strength",
810	                        "method_involved": best_method,
811	                        "observation": (
812	                            f"{best_method.replace('_', ' ').title()} reasoning is particularly "
813	                            f"effective in {domain} domain with {method_stats['accuracy']:.1%} accuracy."
814	                        ),
815	                        "domain": domain,
816	                        "confidence": min(method_stats["resolved"] / 10.0, 1.0),
817	                        "evidence": {
818	                            "accuracy": method_stats["accuracy"],
819	                            "resolved_count": method_stats["resolved"],
820	                        },
821	                        "recommendation": (
822	                            f"Prioritize {best_method.replace('_', ' ')} reasoning "
823	                            f"for future {domain} analysis."
824	                        ),
825	                    })
826	
827	        # 4. Method combination feedback
828	        # Look for cases where combining methods might help
829	        if len(report) > 1:
830	            high_variance = [
831	                (m, s) for m, s in report.items()
832	                if s["resolved_conclusions"] > 2
833	                and 0.45 <= (s["accuracy_rate"] or 0.5) <= 0.65
834	            ]
835	
836	            if high_variance:
837	                feedback.append({
838	                    "feedback_type": "combination",
839	                    "method_involved": [m for m, s in high_variance],
840	                    "observation": (
841	                        f"Several methods show moderate performance "
842	                        f"({[m for m, s in high_variance]}). "
843	                        f"Combining complementary methods may improve reliability."
844	                    ),
845	                    "domain": "all",
846	                    "confidence": 0.6,
847	                    "evidence": {
848	                        "moderate_methods": [
849	                            {"method": m, "accuracy": s["accuracy_rate"]}
850	                            for m, s in high_variance
851	                        ]
852	                    },
853	                    "recommendation": (
854	                        f"When using {', '.join([m for m, s in high_variance])}, "
855	                        f"implement cross-validation with a second complementary method."
856	                    ),
857	                })
858	
859	        return feedback
860	
861	    def _generate_recommendations(
862	        self,
863	        method: str,
864	        overall: MethodAccuracyRecord,
865	        best_domains: List[str],
866	        worst_domains: List[str],
867	        is_calibrated: bool,
868	    ) -> List[str]:
869	        """Helper to generate specific recommendations for a method."""
870	        recommendations = []
871	
872	        if not overall.resolved_conclusions:
873	            recommendations.append(f"Gather more resolved predictions ({overall.resolved_conclusions} so far).")
874	            return recommendations
875	
876	        accuracy = overall.accuracy_rate or 0.0
877	
878	        if accuracy > 0.7:
879	            recommendations.append(f"Excellent track record. Increase reliance in all domains.")
880	            if best_domains:
881	                recommendations.append(f"Particularly strong in: {', '.join(best_domains)}")
882	        elif accuracy < 0.4:
883	            recommendations.append(f"Weak performance. Use only as secondary validation method.")
884	            if worst_domains:
885	                recommendations.append(f"Avoid using in: {', '.join(worst_domains)}")
886	        else:
887	            recommendations.append(f"Moderate performance. Use with caution, validate with other methods.")
888	
889	        if not is_calibrated:
890	            if overall.calibration_error and overall.calibration_error > 0.2:
891	                recommendations.append("Confidence is poorly calibrated. Recalibrate confidence thresholds.")
892	
893	        if best_domains and worst_domains:
894	            recommendations.append(
895	                f"Strong in {best_domains[0]}, weak in {worst_domains[0]}. "
896	                f"Investigate domain-specific factors."
897	            )
898	
899	        return recommendations
900

902	# ── AutoResolver ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
903	
904	class AutoResolver:
905	    """
906	    Semi-automatic resolution of predictions using Claude with web search.
907	
908	    Given a conclusion and the current date, attempts to determine:
909	    - Is this resolvable yet?
910	    - If yes, was it correct?
911	    - What evidence supports the resolution?
912	    """
913	
914	    def __init__(self, llm: LLMClient | None = None):
915	        """
916	        Initialize the resolver.
917	
918	        Args:
919	            llm: Optional LLM client (uses configured default if not provided)
920	        """
921	        self._llm = llm or llm_client_from_settings()
922	
923	    def check_resolvable(
924	        self,
925	        conclusion: SubstantiveConclusion
926	    ) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[bool], str]:
927	        """
928	        Check if a prediction is resolvable and, if so, determine its accuracy.
929	
930	        Uses Claude to reason about whether:
931	        1. The prediction can be checked yet (based on resolution_date)
932	        2. If resolvable, whether it turned out to be correct
933	        3. What evidence supports the determination
934	
935	        Args:
936	            conclusion: The conclusion to check
937	
938	        Returns:
939	            Tuple of (is_resolvable, was_correct, reasoning)
940	            - is_resolvable: True if we can check the prediction now
941	            - was_correct: True/False if resolvable, None if not
942	            - reasoning: Explanation of how it was determined
943	        """
944	        # Check if it's time to resolve
945	        if not conclusion.is_prediction:
946	            return False, None, "Not marked as a prediction"
947	
948	        if conclusion.resolved is not None:
949	            return False, None, "Already resolved"
950	
951	        if conclusion.resolution_date is None:
952	            return False, None, "No resolution date specified"
953	
954	        today = date.today()
955	        if today < conclusion.resolution_date:
956	            days_until = (conclusion.resolution_date - today).days
957	            return False, None, f"Too early to resolve (in {days_until} days)"
958	
959	        # Try to resolve using Claude
960	        try:
961	            prompt = f"""
962	Given this prediction made on {conclusion.episode_date}:
963	
964	"{conclusion.text}"
965	
966	Reasoning method: {conclusion.method_used}
967	Domain: {conclusion.domain}
968	Stated resolution condition: {conclusion.falsification_condition or "Not specified"}
969	Intended resolution date: {conclusion.resolution_date}
970	Today's date: {today}
971	
972	Determine:
973	1. Can this prediction be evaluated now? (Yes/No)
974	2. If yes, was it correct? (True/False/Partially True)
975	3. What evidence or information supports this determination?
976	
977	Format your response as:
978	RESOLVABLE: [Yes/No]
979	CORRECT: [True/False/Partially True/N/A]
980	EVIDENCE: [Brief explanation with sources where applicable]
981	"""
982	
983	            response_text = self._llm.complete(
984	                system="Follow the user's output format exactly.",
985	                user=prompt,
986	                max_tokens=500,
987	                temperature=0.0,
988	            )
989	
990	            # Parse response
991	            lines = response_text.strip().split('\n')
992	            resolvable = False
993	            correct = None
994	            evidence = ""
995	
996	            for line in lines:
997	                if line.startswith("RESOLVABLE:"):
998	                    resolvable = "yes" in line.lower()
999	                elif line.startswith("CORRECT:"):
1000	                    if "true" in line.lower() and "partially" not in line.lower():
1001	                        correct = True
1002	                    elif "false" in line.lower():
1003	                        correct = False
1004	                    # Partially True or N/A → return None
1005	                elif line.startswith("EVIDENCE:"):
1006	                    evidence = line.replace("EVIDENCE:", "").strip()
1007	
1008	            if not evidence:
1009	                evidence = response_text
1010	
1011	            return resolvable, correct, evidence
1012	
1013	        except Exception as e:
1014	            logger.error(f"Error checking resolvable conclusion {conclusion.id}: {e}")
1015	            return False, None, f"Error during resolution check: {str(e)}"
1016

1	"""
2	Founder Attribution and Analysis for Noosphere.
3	
4	The communal brain is composed of individual minds. This module tracks
5	each founder's intellectual identity, their distinctive contributions
6	to the methodological graph, and the dynamics of convergence and
7	divergence among them.
8	
9	Key architectural principle: every claim in the system is attributed
10	to a specific founder. Principles (the distilled, stable nodes in the
11	graph) carry weighted contribution records showing which founders
12	shaped them and how much. This enables:
13	
14	  1. Per-founder intellectual profiles and evolution tracking
15	  2. Convergence/divergence analysis (are founders aligning or splitting?)
16	  3. Weighted principle formation (a principle is stronger when multiple
17	     founders independently converge on it)
18	  4. Written input ingestion with proper author labeling
19	  5. Individual methodological orientation scores
20	
21	The FounderRegistry is the central store. It persists alongside the
22	Ontology Graph and Conclusions Registry.
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import json
28	from collections import defaultdict
29	from datetime import date, datetime
30	from pathlib import Path
31	from typing import Any, Optional, Dict, List, Tuple
32	
33	import numpy as np
34	
35	from noosphere.models import (
36	    FounderProfile,
37	    FounderIntellectualView,
38	    Speaker,
39	    Claim,
40	    Principle,
41	    Discipline,
42	    InputSource,
43	    InputSourceType,
44	    TemporalSnapshot,
45	)
46	
47	from noosphere.observability import get_logger
48	
49	logger = get_logger(__name__)
50	
51	
52	# ── Founder Registry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
53	
54	class FounderRegistry:
55	    """
56	    Persistent registry of all Theseus founders.
57	
58	    Maps Speaker objects (transient, per-episode) to stable FounderProfiles.
59	    Handles name normalisation, profile creation, and persistence.
60	    """
61	
62	    def __init__(self, data_path: str = "founders_registry.json"):
63	        self.data_path = Path(data_path)
64	        self.founders: Dict[str, FounderProfile] = {}
65	        self._name_index: Dict[str, str] = {}  # normalised_name → founder_id
66	        self.load()
67	
68	    # ── Registration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	    def register(
71	        self,
72	        name: str,
73	        role: str = "founder",
74	        primary_domains: Optional[List[Discipline]] = None,
75	        speaker_id: str = "",
76	    ) -> FounderProfile:
77	        """
78	        Register a founder or return existing profile if already known.
79	
80	        Name matching is case-insensitive and whitespace-normalised.
81	        """
82	        norm = self._normalise_name(name)
83	        if norm in self._name_index:
84	            existing = self.founders[self._name_index[norm]]
85	            # Update speaker_id if newly provided
86	            if speaker_id and not existing.speaker_id:
87	                existing.speaker_id = speaker_id
88	                existing.updated_at = datetime.now()
89	            return existing
90	
91	        profile = FounderProfile(
92	            name=name,
93	            speaker_id=speaker_id,
94	            role=role,
95	            primary_domains=primary_domains or [],
96	        )
97	        self.founders[profile.id] = profile
98	        self._name_index[norm] = profile.id
99	        logger.info(f"Registered founder: {name} ({profile.id[:8]}...)")
100	        return profile
101	
102	    def resolve_speaker(self, speaker: Speaker) -> Optional[FounderProfile]:
103	        """
104	        Find the FounderProfile matching a Speaker object.
105	
106	        Tries speaker_id first, then name matching.
107	        """
108	        # Try direct ID match
109	        for fp in self.founders.values():
110	            if fp.speaker_id == speaker.id:
111	                return fp
112	
113	        # Try name match
114	        norm = self._normalise_name(speaker.name)
115	        if norm in self._name_index:
116	            fp = self.founders[self._name_index[norm]]
117	            # Bind the speaker_id for future lookups
118	            if not fp.speaker_id:
119	                fp.speaker_id = speaker.id
120	            return fp
121	
122	        # Auto-register if role is founder
123	        if speaker.role == "founder":
124	            return self.register(
125	                name=speaker.name,
126	                role=speaker.role,
127	                speaker_id=speaker.id,
128	            )
129	
130	        return None
131	
132	    def get_founder(self, founder_id: str) -> Optional[FounderProfile]:
133	        return self.founders.get(founder_id)
134	
135	    def get_by_name(self, name: str) -> Optional[FounderProfile]:
136	        norm = self._normalise_name(name)
137	        fid = self._name_index.get(norm)
138	        return self.founders.get(fid) if fid else None
139	
140	    def all_founders(self) -> List[FounderProfile]:
141	        return list(self.founders.values())
142	
143	    # ── Profile Updates ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
144	
145	    def record_claims(
146	        self,
147	        founder_id: str,
148	        claims: List[Claim],
149	        source_type: InputSourceType = InputSourceType.TRANSCRIPT,
150	        episode_date: Optional[date] = None,
151	    ) -> None:
152	        """
153	        Update a founder's profile after new claims are ingested.
154	
155	        Increments counts, updates activity dates, recalculates
156	        methodological orientation.
157	        """
158	        fp = self.founders.get(founder_id)
159	        if not fp:
160	            logger.warning(f"Founder {founder_id} not found")
161	            return
162	
163	        fp.claim_count += len(claims)
164	
165	        if source_type == InputSourceType.WRITTEN:
166	            fp.written_input_count += 1
167	
168	        # Update activity dates
169	        if episode_date:
170	            if fp.first_appearance is None or episode_date < fp.first_appearance:
171	                fp.first_appearance = episode_date
172	            if fp.last_active is None or episode_date > fp.last_active:
173	                fp.last_active = episode_date
174	
175	        # Recalculate embedding centroid
176	        embeddings = [c.embedding for c in claims if c.embedding]
177	        if embeddings:
178	            new_centroid = np.mean(embeddings, axis=0).tolist()
179	            if fp.embedding_centroid:
180	                # Running average weighted by claim count
181	                old_weight = (fp.claim_count - len(claims)) / fp.claim_count
182	                new_weight = len(claims) / fp.claim_count
183	                fp.embedding_centroid = (
184	                    np.array(fp.embedding_centroid) * old_weight
185	                    + np.array(new_centroid) * new_weight
186	                ).tolist()
187	            else:
188	                fp.embedding_centroid = new_centroid
189	
190	        fp.updated_at = datetime.now()
191	
192	    def record_methodology_counts(
193	        self,
194	        founder_id: str,
195	        methodological: int,
196	        substantive: int,
197	    ) -> None:
198	        """
199	        Update per-founder methodology/substance split after classification.
200	        """
201	        fp = self.founders.get(founder_id)
202	        if not fp:
203	            return
204	
205	        fp.methodological_claim_count += methodological
206	        fp.substantive_claim_count += substantive
207	
208	        total = fp.methodological_claim_count + fp.substantive_claim_count
209	        if total > 0:
210	            fp.methodological_orientation = fp.methodological_claim_count / total
211	
212	        fp.updated_at = datetime.now()
213	
214	    def record_principle_contribution(
215	        self, founder_id: str, principle_id: str
216	    ) -> None:
217	        """Record that a founder contributed to a principle."""
218	        fp = self.founders.get(founder_id)
219	        if fp and principle_id not in fp.principle_ids:
220	            fp.principle_ids.append(principle_id)
221	            fp.updated_at = datetime.now()
222	
223	    # ── Persistence ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
224	
225	    def save(self) -> None:
226	        data = {
227	            "founders": {
228	                fid: fp.model_dump(mode="json")
229	                for fid, fp in self.founders.items()
230	            },
231	            "name_index": self._name_index,
232	            "saved_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
233	        }
234	        with open(self.data_path, "w") as f:
235	            json.dump(data, f, indent=2, default=str)
236	
237	    def load(self) -> None:
238	        if not self.data_path.exists():
239	            return
240	        try:
241	            with open(self.data_path, "r") as f:
242	                data = json.load(f)
243	            for fid, fdata in data.get("founders", {}).items():
244	                self.founders[fid] = FounderProfile(**fdata)
245	            self._name_index = data.get("name_index", {})
246	            logger.info(f"Loaded {len(self.founders)} founder profiles")
247	        except Exception as e:
248	            logger.error(f"Failed to load founder registry: {e}")
249	
250	    # ── Internal ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
251	
252	    @staticmethod
253	    def _normalise_name(name: str) -> str:
254	        return " ".join(name.strip().lower().split())

259	class InputSourceRegistry:
260	    """
261	    Tracks all material that has entered the system.
262	
263	    Each transcript episode and each written document gets an InputSource
264	    record. This enables full provenance tracing: for any claim in the
265	    graph, you can determine where it came from and who authored it.
266	    """
267	
268	    def __init__(self, data_path: str = "input_sources.json"):
269	        self.data_path = Path(data_path)
270	        self.sources: Dict[str, InputSource] = {}
271	        self.load()
272	
273	    def register_transcript(
274	        self,
275	        episode_id: str,
276	        title: str,
277	        episode_date: date,
278	        file_path: str = "",
279	    ) -> InputSource:
280	        """Register a transcript episode as an input source."""
281	        source = InputSource(
282	            source_type=InputSourceType.TRANSCRIPT,
283	            title=title,
284	            episode_id=episode_id,
285	            date=episode_date,
286	            file_path=file_path,
287	        )
288	        self.sources[source.id] = source
289	        return source
290	
291	    def register_written_input(
292	        self,
293	        title: str,
294	        author_id: str,

296	        file_path: str = "",
297	        input_date: Optional[date] = None,
298	        description: str = "",
299	        source_type: InputSourceType = InputSourceType.WRITTEN,
300	    ) -> InputSource:
301	        """Register a written document (essay, memo, notes) as an input source."""
302	        source = InputSource(
303	            source_type=source_type,
304	            title=title,
305	            author_id=author_id,
306	            author_name=author_name,
307	            file_path=file_path,
308	            date=input_date or date.today(),
309	            description=description,
310	        )
311	        self.sources[source.id] = source

314	    def get_source(self, source_id: str) -> Optional[InputSource]:

318	        for s in self.sources.values():

326	            if s.author_id == author_id

329	    def save(self) -> None:
330	        data = {
331	            "sources": {
332	                sid: s.model_dump(mode="json")
333	                for sid, s in self.sources.items()
334	            },
335	            "saved_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
336	        }
337	        with open(self.data_path, "w") as f:
338	            json.dump(data, f, indent=2, default=str)

340	    def load(self) -> None:
341	        if not self.data_path.exists():
342	            return
343	        try:
344	            with open(self.data_path, "r") as f:
345	                data = json.load(f)

347	                self.sources[sid] = InputSource(**sdata)

353	# ── Founder Analyser ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
354	
355	class FounderAnalyser:
356	    """
357	    Analytical engine for inter-founder dynamics.
358	
359	    Computes convergence, divergence, influence asymmetries, and
360	    per-founder intellectual evolution. Operates over the FounderRegistry,
361	    the OntologyGraph, and claim-level data.
362	    """
363	
364	    def __init__(
365	        self,
366	        registry: FounderRegistry,
367	        embedding_model=None,
368	    ):
369	        self.registry = registry
370	        self.model = embedding_model
371	
372	    # ── Convergence / Divergence ────────────────────────────────────────
373	
374	    def pairwise_similarity(self) -> Dict[Tuple[str, str], float]:
375	        """
376	        Compute pairwise cosine similarity between all founder centroids.
377	
378	        Returns:
379	            Dict mapping (founder_id_a, founder_id_b) → cosine similarity.
380	            Only founders with computed centroids are included.
381	        """
382	        founders = [
383	            f for f in self.registry.all_founders()
384	            if f.embedding_centroid is not None
385	        ]
386	        if len(founders) < 2:
387	            return {}
388	
389	        results = {}
390	        for i, a in enumerate(founders):
391	            for b in founders[i + 1:]:
392	                vec_a = np.array(a.embedding_centroid)
393	                vec_b = np.array(b.embedding_centroid)
394	                cos_sim = float(
395	                    np.dot(vec_a, vec_b)
396	                    / (np.linalg.norm(vec_a) * np.linalg.norm(vec_b) + 1e-9)
397	                )
398	                results[(a.id, b.id)] = cos_sim
399	
400	        return results
401	
402	    def convergence_report(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
403	        """
404	        Comprehensive convergence/divergence analysis across all founders.
405	
406	        Returns a report with:
407	          - Overall convergence score (mean pairwise similarity)
408	          - Most aligned pair
409	          - Most divergent pair
410	          - Per-founder isolation score (how far from the group centroid)
411	        """
412	        sims = self.pairwise_similarity()
413	        if not sims:
414	            return {"status": "insufficient_data", "founders": len(self.registry.all_founders())}
415	
416	        similarities = list(sims.values())
417	        overall = float(np.mean(similarities))
418	
419	        most_aligned = max(sims.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
420	        most_divergent = min(sims.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
421	
422	        # Group centroid
423	        centroids = [
424	            np.array(f.embedding_centroid)
425	            for f in self.registry.all_founders()
426	            if f.embedding_centroid is not None
427	        ]
428	        if centroids:
429	            group_centroid = np.mean(centroids, axis=0)
430	        else:
431	            group_centroid = None
432	
433	        # Per-founder isolation
434	        isolation = {}
435	        if group_centroid is not None:
436	            for f in self.registry.all_founders():
437	                if f.embedding_centroid is not None:
438	                    vec = np.array(f.embedding_centroid)
439	                    cos_sim = float(
440	                        np.dot(vec, group_centroid)
441	                        / (np.linalg.norm(vec) * np.linalg.norm(group_centroid) + 1e-9)
442	                    )
443	                    isolation[f.name] = round(1.0 - cos_sim, 4)
444	
445	        return {
446	            "overall_convergence": round(overall, 4),
447	            "most_aligned": {
448	                "pair": (
449	                    self.registry.get_founder(most_aligned[0][0]).name,
450	                    self.registry.get_founder(most_aligned[0][1]).name,
451	                ),
452	                "similarity": round(most_aligned[1], 4),
453	            },
454	            "most_divergent": {
455	                "pair": (
456	                    self.registry.get_founder(most_divergent[0][0]).name,
457	                    self.registry.get_founder(most_divergent[0][1]).name,
458	                ),
459	                "similarity": round(most_divergent[1], 4),
460	            },
461	            "per_founder_isolation": isolation,
462	            "num_founders": len(self.registry.all_founders()),
463	        }
464	
465	    # ── Principle Contribution Analysis ─────────────────────────────────
466	
467	    def principle_authorship_report(
468	        self,
469	        principles: Dict[str, Principle],
470	    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
471	        """
472	        Analyse how principles are distributed across founders.
473	
474	        Reports:
475	          - Per-founder: which principles they shaped, total influence weight
476	          - Per-principle: which founders contributed, concentration (Herfindahl)
477	          - Collaborative vs. individual principles
478	        """
479	        founder_influence: Dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
480	        founder_principles: Dict[str, List[str]] = defaultdict(list)
481	        principle_concentration: Dict[str, float] = {}
482	        collaborative = []
483	        individual = []
484	
485	        for pid, p in principles.items():
486	            contribs = p.founder_contributions
487	            if not contribs:
488	                continue
489	
490	            # Per-founder aggregation
491	            for fid, weight in contribs.items():
492	                founder_influence[fid] += weight
493	                founder_principles[fid].append(pid)
494	
495	            # Herfindahl index: sum of squared shares
496	            # HHI = 1.0 means one founder dominates; 1/N means equal split
497	            shares = list(contribs.values())
498	            total = sum(shares)
499	            if total > 0:
500	                normalised = [s / total for s in shares]
501	                hhi = sum(s ** 2 for s in normalised)
502	                principle_concentration[pid] = round(hhi, 4)
503	
504	                if len(contribs) > 1 and hhi < 0.5:
505	                    collaborative.append(pid)
506	                else:
507	                    individual.append(pid)
508	
509	        # Build per-founder report
510	        per_founder = {}
511	        for fid in founder_influence:
512	            fp = self.registry.get_founder(fid)
513	            name = fp.name if fp else fid[:8]
514	            per_founder[name] = {
515	                "total_influence": round(founder_influence[fid], 3),
516	                "principle_count": len(founder_principles[fid]),
517	                "methodological_orientation": round(
518	                    fp.methodological_orientation, 3
519	                ) if fp else None,
520	            }
521	
522	        return {
523	            "per_founder": per_founder,
524	            "principle_concentration": principle_concentration,
525	            "collaborative_principles": len(collaborative),
526	            "individual_principles": len(individual),
527	            "total_principles": len(principles),
528	        }
529	
530	    # ── Per-Founder Profile Summaries ───────────────────────────────────
531	
532	    def founder_profile_summary(self, founder_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
533	        """Detailed summary of a single founder's intellectual profile."""
534	        fp = self.registry.get_founder(founder_id)
535	        if not fp:
536	            return {"error": f"Founder {founder_id} not found"}
537	
538	        total_claims = fp.methodological_claim_count + fp.substantive_claim_count
539	
540	        return {
541	            "name": fp.name,
542	            "id": fp.id,
543	            "role": fp.role,
544	            "primary_domains": [d.value for d in fp.primary_domains],
545	            "claim_count": fp.claim_count,
546	            "written_inputs": fp.written_input_count,
547	            "methodological_claims": fp.methodological_claim_count,
548	            "substantive_claims": fp.substantive_claim_count,
549	            "methodological_orientation": round(fp.methodological_orientation, 3),
550	            "principles_shaped": len(fp.principle_ids),
551	            "avg_conviction": round(fp.avg_conviction_score, 3),
552	            "first_appearance": fp.first_appearance.isoformat() if fp.first_appearance else None,
553	            "last_active": fp.last_active.isoformat() if fp.last_active else None,
554	            "has_centroid": fp.embedding_centroid is not None,
555	        }
556	
557	    def all_profiles_summary(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
558	        """Summary of all registered founders."""
559	        return [
560	            self.founder_profile_summary(fid)
561	            for fid in self.registry.founders
562	        ]
563	
564	    # ── Method Preference Analysis ──────────────────────────────────────
565	
566	    def method_preferences(
567	        self, claims_by_founder: Dict[str, List[Claim]]
568	    ) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, int]]:
569	        """
570	        For each founder, compute domain distribution of their claims.
571	
572	        Returns:
573	            Dict[founder_name, Dict[discipline_name, count]]
574	        """
575	        result = {}
576	        for fid, claims in claims_by_founder.items():
577	            fp = self.registry.get_founder(fid)
578	            name = fp.name if fp else fid[:8]
579	            domain_counts: Dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
580	            for c in claims:
581	                for d in c.disciplines:
582	                    domain_counts[d.value] += 1
583	            result[name] = dict(
584	                sorted(domain_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])
585	            )
586	        return result
587	
588	
589	# ── Written Input Processor ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
590	
591	class WrittenInputProcessor:
592	    """
593	    Handles ingestion of written documents (essays, memos, research notes).
594	
595	    Unlike transcripts, written inputs have a single unambiguous author.
596	    The processor reads the document, extracts claims using the same
597	    ClaimExtractor pipeline, labels every claim with the author's
598	    FounderProfile, and returns enriched claims ready for the orchestrator.
599	    """
600	
601	    def __init__(
602	        self,
603	        founder_registry: FounderRegistry,
604	        source_registry: InputSourceRegistry,
605	        claim_extractor=None,
606	        embedding_model=None,
607	        logger_instance=None,
608	    ):
609	        self.founder_registry = founder_registry
610	        self.source_registry = source_registry
611	        self._claim_extractor = claim_extractor
612	        self._embedding_model = embedding_model
613	        self.logger = logger_instance or logger
614	
615	    @property
616	    def claim_extractor(self):
617	        if self._claim_extractor is None:
618	            from noosphere.ingester import ClaimExtractor, ExtractionConfig
619	            self._claim_extractor = ClaimExtractor(
620	                config=ExtractionConfig(),
621	                logger_instance=self.logger,
622	            )
623	        return self._claim_extractor
624	
625	    @property
626	    def embedding_model(self):
627	        if self._embedding_model is None:
628	            from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
629	
630	            from noosphere.config import get_settings
631	
632	            s = get_settings()
633	            kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}
634	            if s.embedding_device:
635	                kwargs["device"] = s.embedding_device
636	            self._embedding_model = SentenceTransformer(
637	                s.embedding_model_name, **kwargs
638	            )
639	        return self._embedding_model
640	
641	    def ingest_written_input(
642	        self,
643	        file_path: str,
644	        author_name: str,
645	        title: str = "",
646	        input_date: Optional[date] = None,
647	        description: str = "",
648	        source_type: InputSourceType = InputSourceType.WRITTEN,
649	    ) -> Tuple[List[Claim], InputSource]:
650	        """
651	        Ingest a written document from a specific founder.
652	
653	        The document is read, claims are extracted with Claude, and every
654	        claim is labeled with the author's FounderProfile and InputSource.
655	
656	        Args:
657	            file_path: Path to the document (.txt, .md, .pdf, etc.)
658	            author_name: Name of the founder who wrote it
659	            title: Document title
660	            input_date: When it was written (defaults to today)
661	            description: Brief description of the document
662	            source_type: WRITTEN, ANNOTATION, or EXTERNAL
663	
664	        Returns:
665	            Tuple of (List[Claim], InputSource)
666	        """
667	        path = Path(file_path)
668	        if not path.exists():
669	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Document not found: {path}")
670	
671	        self.logger.info(f"Ingesting written input: {title or path.name} by {author_name}")
672	
673	        # Resolve or register the founder
674	        founder = self.founder_registry.register(name=author_name)
675	
676	        # Register the input source
677	        source = self.source_registry.register_written_input(
678	            title=title or path.stem,
679	            author_id=founder.id,
680	            author_name=author_name,
681	            file_path=str(path),
682	            input_date=input_date,
683	            description=description,
684	            source_type=source_type,
685	        )
686	
687	        # Read the document
688	        text = self._read_document(path)
689	
690	        # Convert to transcript segments for the claim extractor
691	        # Written inputs are treated as a single long segment from one speaker
692	        from noosphere.models import TranscriptSegment
693	        speaker = Speaker(name=author_name, role="founder", id=founder.speaker_id or str(founder.id))
694	        segments = self._split_into_segments(text, speaker, source.id)
695	
696	        # Extract claims
697	        claims = self.claim_extractor.extract(segments)
698	
699	        # Enrich claims with author attribution and embeddings
700	        actual_date = input_date or date.today()
701	        enriched = []
702	        for claim in claims:
703	            claim.founder_id = founder.id
704	            claim.source_type = source_type
705	            claim.source_id = source.id
706	            claim.episode_id = f"written_{source.id[:8]}"
707	            claim.episode_date = actual_date
708	
709	            # Generate embedding
710	            if self._embedding_model or self.embedding_model:
711	                try:
712	                    claim.embedding = self.embedding_model.encode(claim.text).tolist()
713	                except Exception as e:
714	                    self.logger.warning(f"Embedding failed for claim: {e}")
715	
716	            enriched.append(claim)
717	
718	        # Update founder profile
719	        self.founder_registry.record_claims(
720	            founder_id=founder.id,
721	            claims=enriched,
722	            source_type=source_type,
723	            episode_date=actual_date,
724	        )
725	
726	        self.logger.info(
727	            f"Extracted {len(enriched)} claims from {title or path.name} by {author_name}"
728	        )
729	
730	        return enriched, source
731	
732	    def _read_document(self, path: Path) -> str:
733	        """Read a document file. Supports .txt, .md, and plain text."""
734	        suffix = path.suffix.lower()
735	
736	        if suffix in (".txt", ".md", ".text", ".markdown"):
737	            return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
738	
739	        elif suffix == ".pdf":
740	            # Attempt PDF extraction
741	            try:
742	                import subprocess
743	                result = subprocess.run(
744	                    ["pdftotext", str(path), "-"],
745	                    capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30,
746	                )
747	                if result.returncode == 0:
748	                    return result.stdout
749	            except Exception:
750	                pass
751	
752	            # Fallback: try pypdf
753	            try:
754	                from pypdf import PdfReader
755	                reader = PdfReader(str(path))
756	                return "\n".join(page.extract_text() or "" for page in reader.pages)
757	            except ImportError:
758	                raise ImportError(
759	                    "PDF reading requires 'pypdf'. Install with: pip install pypdf"
760	                )
761	
762	        elif suffix in (".docx",):
763	            try:
764	                from docx import Document
765	                doc = Document(str(path))
766	                return "\n\n".join(p.text for p in doc.paragraphs if p.text.strip())
767	            except ImportError:
768	                raise ImportError(
769	                    "DOCX reading requires 'python-docx'. Install with: pip install python-docx"
770	                )
771	
772	        else:
773	            # Try reading as plain text
774	            return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
775	
776	    def _split_into_segments(
777	        self,
778	        text: str,
779	        speaker: Speaker,
780	        source_id: str,
781	    ) -> List[TranscriptSegment]:
782	        """
783	        Split written text into manageable segments for claim extraction.
784	
785	        Written text is split on paragraph boundaries. Segments shorter
786	        than 50 characters are merged with the next one.
787	        """
788	        from noosphere.models import TranscriptSegment
789	
790	        paragraphs = [p.strip() for p in text.split("\n\n") if p.strip()]
791	
792	        # Merge short paragraphs
793	        merged = []
794	        buffer = ""
795	        for para in paragraphs:
796	            if buffer:
797	                buffer += "\n\n" + para
798	            else:
799	                buffer = para
800	
801	            if len(buffer) >= 200:  # Minimum segment length
802	                merged.append(buffer)
803	                buffer = ""
804	
805	        if buffer:
806	            if merged:
807	                merged[-1] += "\n\n" + buffer
808	            else:
809	                merged.append(buffer)
810	
811	        return [
812	            TranscriptSegment(
813	                speaker=speaker,
814	                text=seg,
815	                episode_id=f"written_{source_id[:8]}",
816	            )
817	            for seg in merged
818	        ]
819	
820	
821	# ── Per-founder intellectual view (Phase 4) ─────────────────────────────────
822	
823	
824	def _normalise_speaker(n: str) -> str:
825	    return " ".join(n.lower().split())
826	
827	
828	def compute_founder_intellectual_view(
829	    *,
830	    founder: FounderProfile,
831	    claims: List[Claim],
832	    topic_for_claim: Any,
833	    drift_events: Optional[List[Any]] = None,
834	) -> FounderIntellectualView:
835	    """
836	    Aggregate positions by topic, drift hits, cross-founder tension, sole-dissenter topics.
837	
838	    ``topic_for_claim`` is ``Callable[[Claim], Optional[str]]`` (e.g. store.get_topic_id_for_claim).
839	    ``drift_events`` is a list of ``DriftEvent``-like objects with ``author_topic_key`` / ``notes``.
840	    """
841	    drift_events = drift_events or []
842	    name_key = _normalise_speaker(founder.name)
843	    positions: Dict[str, str] = {}
844	    topic_claims: Dict[str, List[Claim]] = defaultdict(list)
845	    for c in claims:
846	        if _normalise_speaker(c.speaker.name) != name_key:
847	            continue
848	        tid = topic_for_claim(c) if callable(topic_for_claim) else None
849	        if not tid:
850	            tid = "_untagged"
851	        topic_claims[tid].append(c)
852	    for tid, lst in topic_claims.items():
853	        snippet = lst[-1].text[:240].replace("\n", " ")
854	        positions[tid] = snippet
855	
856	    drift_ids: List[str] = []
857	    for ev in drift_events:
858	        key = getattr(ev, "author_topic_key", "") or getattr(ev, "notes", "")
859	        if name_key in _normalise_speaker(str(key)):
860	            drift_ids.append(getattr(ev, "id", ""))
861	
862	    edges: List[str] = []
863	    my_claims = [c for c in claims if _normalise_speaker(c.speaker.name) == name_key]
864	    others = [c for c in claims if _normalise_speaker(c.speaker.name) != name_key]
865	    import numpy as np
866	
867	    for a in my_claims[:40]:
868	        if not a.embedding:
869	            continue
870	        va = np.asarray(a.embedding, dtype=float)
871	        va = va / (np.linalg.norm(va) + 1e-9)
872	        for b in others[:80]:
873	            if not b.embedding:
874	                continue
875	            vb = np.asarray(b.embedding, dtype=float)
876	            vb = vb / (np.linalg.norm(vb) + 1e-9)
877	            if va.shape != vb.shape:
878	                continue
879	            if float(np.dot(va, vb)) < -0.15:
880	                edges.append(f"{a.id}|{b.id}")
881	
882	    sole_topics: List[str] = []
883	    for tid, lst in topic_claims.items():
884	        if tid == "_untagged":
885	            continue
886	        authors = { _normalise_speaker(c.speaker.name) for c in claims if (topic_for_claim(c) if callable(topic_for_claim) else None) == tid }
887	        if len(authors) > 1 and _normalise_speaker(founder.name) in authors:
888	            others_t = authors - { _normalise_speaker(founder.name) }
889	            if not others_t:
890	                continue
891	            my_cent = np.mean([np.asarray(c.embedding, dtype=float) for c in lst if c.embedding], axis=0)
892	            o_vecs = [
893	                np.asarray(c.embedding, dtype=float)
894	                for c in claims
895	                if (topic_for_claim(c) if callable(topic_for_claim) else None) == tid
896	                and _normalise_speaker(c.speaker.name) in others_t
897	                and c.embedding
898	            ]
899	            if not len(o_vecs):
900	                continue
901	            oc = np.mean(o_vecs, axis=0)
902	            my_cent = my_cent / (np.linalg.norm(my_cent) + 1e-9)
903	            oc = oc / (np.linalg.norm(oc) + 1e-9)
904	            if float(np.dot(my_cent, oc)) < 0.2:
905	                sole_topics.append(tid)
906	
907	    return FounderIntellectualView(
908	        founder_id=founder.id,
909	        founder_name=founder.name,
910	        positions_by_topic=positions,
911	        drift_event_ids=[x for x in drift_ids if x],
912	        cross_founder_contradiction_edges=edges[:50],
913	        sole_dissenter_topics=sole_topics,
914	    )

1	"""
2	External literature ingestion — connectors produce ``Artifact`` + ``Chunk`` rows,
3	``Claim`` rows with ``claim_origin=literature`` (first author as ``Voice``).
4	
5	Copyright: respect ``license_status``; full text is only stored when access is lawful.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import hashlib
11	import json
12	import re
13	import tempfile
14	import uuid
15	import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
16	from dataclasses import dataclass
17	from datetime import date, datetime, timezone
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Any, Optional, Protocol, runtime_checkable
20	
21	import requests
22	
23	from noosphere.ids import artifact_id_from_bytes, chunk_id
24	from noosphere.models import Artifact, Chunk, Claim, ClaimOrigin, ClaimType, InputSourceType, Speaker
25	from noosphere.observability import get_logger
26	from noosphere.store import Store
27	from noosphere.voices import ensure_voice_profile, register_voice_corpus_artifact
28	
29	logger = get_logger(__name__)
30	
31	NS = {"a": "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"}
32	
33	
34	@runtime_checkable
35	class SourceConnector(Protocol):
36	    name: str
37	
38	    def ingest(self, store: Store, **kwargs: Any) -> list[str]:
39	        """Return list of new artifact ids."""
40	        ...
41	
42	
43	def _first_author(author_field: str) -> str:
44	    s = (author_field or "").strip()
45	    if not s:
46	        return "Unknown author"
47	    part = re.split(r"[,;]|\band\b", s, maxsplit=1, flags=re.I)[0].strip()
48	    return part or "Unknown author"
49	
50	
51	def _section_type_for_paragraph(para: str) -> str:
52	    u = para.strip()[:80].upper()
53	    if u.startswith("ABSTRACT"):
54	        return "abstract"
55	    if u.startswith("INTRODUCTION") or u.startswith("1 INTRODUCTION"):
56	        return "introduction"
57	    if "METHOD" in u[:40]:
58	        return "methods"
59	    if "RESULT" in u[:40]:
60	        return "results"
61	    if "CONCLUSION" in u[:40]:
62	        return "conclusion"
63	    if "REFERENCE" in u[:40]:
64	        return "references"
65	    return "body"
66	
67	
68	def extract_pdf_text(path: Path) -> str:
69	    try:
70	        from pypdf import PdfReader
71	    except ImportError as e:  # pragma: no cover
72	        raise RuntimeError("pypdf is required for PDF ingestion") from e
73	    reader = PdfReader(str(path))
74	    parts: list[str] = []
75	    for page in reader.pages:
76	        t = page.extract_text() or ""
77	        if t.strip():
78	            parts.append(t)
79	    return "\n\n".join(parts)
80	
81	
82	def _chunks_from_text(
83	    text: str,
84	    artifact_id: str,
85	    *,
86	    max_chars: int = 1800,
87	) -> list[Chunk]:
88	    chunks: list[Chunk] = []
89	    offset = 0
90	    for para in re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", text):
91	        p = para.strip()
92	        if len(p) < 80:
93	            offset += len(para) + 2
94	            continue
95	        section = _section_type_for_paragraph(p)
96	        while p:
97	            piece = p[:max_chars]
98	            p = p[max_chars:]
99	            start = offset
100	            end = start + len(piece)
101	            cid = chunk_id(artifact_id, start, end)
102	            chunks.append(
103	                Chunk(
104	                    id=cid,
105	                    artifact_id=artifact_id,
106	                    start_offset=start,
107	                    end_offset=end,
108	                    text=piece,
109	                    metadata={"section_type": section},
110	                )
111	            )
112	            offset = end + 2
113	        offset += 2
114	    return chunks
115	
116	
117	def _claims_from_chunks(
118	    store: Store,
119	    chunks: list[Chunk],
120	    *,
121	    voice_id: str,
122	    voice_name: str,
123	    artifact_id: str,
124	    pub_date: date,
125	) -> int:
126	    spk = Speaker(name=voice_name, role="author")
127	    n = 0
128	    for ch in chunks:
129	        if len(ch.text.strip()) < 60:
130	            continue
131	        cid = f"lit_{artifact_id[:8]}_{ch.id}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
132	        c = Claim(
133	            id=cid,
134	            text=ch.text.strip()[:4000],
135	            speaker=spk,
136	            episode_id=f"literature:{artifact_id}",
137	            episode_date=pub_date,
138	            claim_type=ClaimType.METHODOLOGICAL,
139	            chunk_id=ch.id,
140	            segment_context=ch.text[:500],
141	            claim_origin=ClaimOrigin.LITERATURE,
142	            voice_id=voice_id,
143	            source_type=InputSourceType.EXTERNAL,
144	            source_id=artifact_id,
145	            source_span_start=ch.start_offset,
146	            source_span_end=ch.end_offset,
147	        )
148	        store.put_claim(c)
149	        n += 1
150	    return n
151	
152	
153	@dataclass
154	class LiteratureIngestResult:
155	    artifact_id: str
156	    voice_id: str
157	    claims_written: int
158	
159	
160	def ingest_literature_text(
161	    store: Store,
162	    *,
163	    title: str,
164	    author: str,
165	    body: str,
166	    connector: str,
167	    license_status: str,
168	    pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
169	    uri: str = "",
170	    mime_type: str = "text/plain",
171	) -> LiteratureIngestResult:
172	    raw = body.encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
173	    if not raw.strip():
174	        raw = f"{title}|{uri}|{connector}".encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
175	    aid = artifact_id_from_bytes(raw)
176	    voice_name = _first_author(author)
177	    voice = ensure_voice_profile(store, voice_name, copyright_status=f"literature:{connector}")
178	    voice = register_voice_corpus_artifact(store, voice, aid)
179	    pd = pub_date or date.today()
180	    art = Artifact(
181	        id=aid,
182	        uri=uri or f"literature:{connector}:{aid}",
183	        mime_type=mime_type,
184	        byte_length=len(raw),
185	        content_sha256=hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest(),
186	        title=title,
187	        author=author,
188	        source_date=pd,
189	        license_status=license_status,
190	        literature_connector=connector,
191	    )
192	    store.put_artifact(art)
193	    chunks = _chunks_from_text(body, aid)
194	    for ch in chunks:
195	        store.put_chunk(ch)
196	    n = _claims_from_chunks(store, chunks, voice_id=voice.id, voice_name=voice.canonical_name, artifact_id=aid, pub_date=pd)
197	    logger.info("literature_ingested", artifact_id=aid, connector=connector, claims=n)
198	    return LiteratureIngestResult(aid, voice.id, n)
199

201	class LocalPDFConnector:
202	    name = "local_pdf"
203	
204	    def ingest(
205	        self,
206	        store: Store,
207	        *,
208	        path: str | Path,
209	        license_status: str = "firm_licensed",
210	        title: str = "",
211	        author: str = "",
212	        pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
213	    ) -> list[str]:
214	        p = Path(path)
215	        text = extract_pdf_text(p)
216	        if license_status == "restricted_metadata_only":
217	            text = text[:0]
218	        meta_path = p.with_suffix(".json")
219	        if meta_path.is_file():
220	            try:
221	                meta = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
222	                title = str(meta.get("title", title or p.stem))
223	                author = str(meta.get("author", author or ""))
224	                if meta.get("date"):
225	                    pub_date = date.fromisoformat(str(meta["date"])[:10])
226	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
227	                pass
228	        if not title:
229	            title = p.stem
230	        res = ingest_literature_text(
231	            store,
232	            title=title,
233	            author=author or "Unknown",
234	            body=text or "(no extractable text — add OCR or text layer)",
235	            connector=self.name,
236	            license_status=license_status,
237	            pub_date=pub_date,
238	            uri=str(p.resolve()),
239	            mime_type="application/pdf",
240	        )
241	        return [res.artifact_id]
242

244	class ManualConnector:
245	    name = "manual"
246	
247	    def ingest(
248	        self,
249	        store: Store,
250	        *,
251	        path: str | Path,
252	        license_status: str = "firm_licensed",
253	        title: str = "",
254	        author: str = "",
255	        pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
256	    ) -> list[str]:
257	        p = Path(path)
258	        suf = p.suffix.lower()
259	        if suf == ".pdf":
260	            return LocalPDFConnector().ingest(
261	                store, path=p, license_status=license_status, title=title, author=author, pub_date=pub_date
262	            )
263	        body = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
264	        res = ingest_literature_text(
265	            store,
266	            title=title or p.stem,
267	            author=author or "Unknown",
268	            body=body,
269	            connector=self.name,
270	            license_status=license_status,
271	            pub_date=pub_date,
272	            uri=str(p.resolve()),
273	            mime_type="text/markdown" if suf in {".md", ".markdown"} else "text/plain",
274	        )
275	        return [res.artifact_id]
